title: New Braunfels
name: New Braunfels
metaTitle: New Braunfels in San Antonio, TX
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for New Braunfels retail, hospitality-adjacent, and flex property on the I-35 corridor, matched to your intermediary's closing timeline.

sections.html: New Braunfels sits along the I-35 corridor between San Antonio and Austin, and 1031 exchange investors increasingly look here for replacement property once the 45-day identification window is running. Coordination in this submarket covers retail, hospitality-adjacent, and light industrial assets tied to Comal and Guadalupe county growth, with every file assembled to match what the qualified intermediary and the investor's tax advisor will require at closing.

sections.heading: New Braunfels Asset Stock and Corridor Positioning
sections.html:
Retail and hospitality-adjacent product cluster near the Gruene Historic District and the Guadalupe and Comal rivers, where visitor traffic supports restaurant pads, tourism-facing retail, and short-term lodging support space. Along the I-35 frontage roads, light industrial and flex buildings serve companies that need middle-mile access between San Antonio and Austin without paying Austin land prices. Multifamily has followed the county's population growth, and area medical facilities have pulled medical office and urgent-care leasing into the mix.

For exchange purposes, all of these fall within the broad like-kind scope for real property held for investment or business use, so an investor exiting a multifamily asset elsewhere can identify a New Braunfels retail pad or flex building without a like-kind classification problem.

sections.heading: Demand Drivers Along the I-35 Corridor
sections.html:
Continued in-migration to Comal and Guadalupe counties keeps rooftop-driven retail demand ahead of new supply in several New Braunfels submarkets, and the absence of a state income tax remains a factor cited by relocating businesses and residents alike. Tourism revenue tied to the rivers and Gruene supports a base of hospitality-adjacent retail that doesn't depend solely on local rooftops.

Distribution and flex demand is driven by companies positioning inventory between the San Antonio and Austin metros rather than committing to either downtown market, which keeps I-35 frontage parcels in active use.

sections.heading: Closing Coordination and Identification Timing in New Braunfels
sections.html:
Because the 45-day identification window does not extend for local permitting or survey delays, New Braunfels files move fastest when documentation is assembled before a property is formally identified. Coordination typically covers:

  • Written identification language meeting Treasury Regulation 1.1031(k)-1 unambiguous-description requirements
  • Comal or Guadalupe County flood-zone and floodplain determination for river-adjacent parcels
  • Purchase contract review for assignment and exchange-cooperation language
  • Lender preflight on debt replacement to avoid a boot exposure at closing
  • Title commitment and survey items flagged for the qualified intermediary before the 180-day exchange period runs out

None of this substitutes for the investor's own legal or tax advice; it is process support to keep the file moving inside the statutory windows.

sections.heading: Watchouts for Investors Trading Into New Braunfels
sections.html:
River-adjacent parcels near the Guadalupe and Comal often carry floodplain overlays that can eliminate a property from consideration after it has already been named in a written identification, which is why flood-zone review belongs before identification, not after. Hospitality-adjacent retail income can swing with tourism seasonality, which matters for lenders sizing debt on a replacement purchase.

Entitlement and permitting timelines with Comal County and the City of New Braunfels can run longer than an investor expects, and competition from other I-35 corridor buyers has compressed pricing on stabilized retail, so comparable analysis should be current, not carried over from a prior search.

sections.heading: Working with a Qualified Intermediary on New Braunfels Property
sections.html: The qualified intermediary holds exchange proceeds and prepares the exchange agreement and assignment documents required to keep the transaction from constructive receipt; that role does not change because the replacement asset sits in New Braunfels rather than downtown San Antonio. If a directly identified property falls out of contract, proceeds can be redirected toward a DST placement as a backup, provided the reinvestment still closes inside the 180-day exchange period. Investors should confirm structuring questions, including boot calculation on any debt or cash difference, with their own tax advisor before the exchange agreement is signed.

faqs.q: Does New Braunfels retail or flex property qualify as like-kind for an exchange out of a different asset class?
faqs.a: Yes. Real property held for investment or business use is broadly like-kind to other real property held for the same purpose, so a New Braunfels retail pad or flex building can replace multifamily, office, or industrial property from another market. Your tax advisor should confirm the specific facts of your file before you rely on this.

faqs.q: How does floodplain exposure near the Guadalupe and Comal rivers affect my 45-day window?
faqs.a: Flood-zone determination should happen before a property is named in your written identification, not after, since a floodplain issue discovered late can force you to substitute or add a property under the three-property or 200% rule with no additional time added to the window.

faqs.q: Can New Braunfels exchange proceeds go into a DST if my direct purchase doesn't close?
faqs.a: Yes, DST placement coordination is available as a fallback, but the DST interest still has to close inside your 180-day exchange period like any other replacement property.

faqs.q: Who drafts the purchase contract for a New Braunfels replacement property?
faqs.a: Your own broker or counsel drafts and negotiates the contract. Coordination on our end focuses on documentation assembly, exchange-cooperation language, and liaison with your qualified intermediary, not contract drafting or legal advice.

faqs.q: If a New Braunfels seller needs more time to close, does my identification deadline move?
faqs.a: No. The 45-day identification window is fixed by the exchange start date regardless of how long any individual closing takes, which is why backup identifications under the three-property or 200% rule are worth keeping in reserve.
